title: Polysaccharide coated nanoparticle compositions comprising ions
abstract: A composition including a coated nanoparticle including a nanoparticle and a cross-linked carbohydrate-based coating and an ion selected from the group consisting of Li + , Na + , K + , Rb + , Cs + , Be2 + , Mg2 + , Ca2 + , Sr2 + , Ba2 + , and mixtures thereof; methods of making and using the composition; and systems including the composition.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS 1. A method of treating a subterranean formation, the method comprising: placing in a subterranean formation a nanoparticle composition comprising: a coated nanoparticle comprising a nanoparticle and a cross-linked carbohydrate- based coating; and an ion selected from the group consisting of Li + , Na " , K + , b + * Cs + , Be 2+ , Mg z+ , Ca 1+ , Sr 2+ , Ba" , and mixtures thereof. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the composition further comprises an aqueous liquid. 3. The method of any one of claims 1-2, wherein the aqueous liquid comprises at least one of water, brine, produced water, flowback water, brackish water, Arab-D-brine, and sea water. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the aqueous liquid comprises at least one of a drilling fluid, a fracturing fluid, a diverting fluid, and a lost circulation treatment fluid, 5. The method of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the method further comprises obtaining or providing the composition, wherein the obtaining or providing of the composition occurs above- surface. 6. The method of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the method further comprises obtaining or providing the composition, wherein the obtaining or providing of the composition occurs in the subterranean formation. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the nanoparticle is a metal oxide nanoparticle. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the nanoparticle is an iron oxide nanoparticle, a nickel oxide nanoparticle, or a cobalt oxide nanoparticle. 9. The method of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the nanoparticle comprises a metal oxide comprising an atom selected from the group consisting of Zn, Cr, Co, Dy, Er, Eu, Gd, Gd, Pr, Nd, In, Pr, Sm, Tb, Tm, and combinations thereof. 10. The method of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the nanoparticle is a superparamagnetic nanoparticle. 11. The method of any one of claims 1-10, wherein the nanoparticle comprises a superparamagnetic nanoparticle selected from the group consisting of iron oxides, pure metals, spinel-type ferromagnets, alloys, and combinations thereof. 12. The method of any one of claims 1-1 1, wherein the superparamagnetic nanoparticle comprises an iron oxide nanoparticle. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the nanoparticle has an average particle size of about 10 nm to about 1,000 nm. 14. The method of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the cross-linked carbohydrate-based coating comprises a carbohydrate selected from the group consisting of a monosaccharide, an oligosaccharide, a polysaccharide, and combinations thereof 15. The method of any one of claims 1-14, wherein the cross-linked carbohydrate-based coating comprises a polysaccharide. 16. The method of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the polysaccharide is selected from the group consisting of a alginate, a chitosan, a curdlan, a dextran, a derivatized dextran, an emulsan, a galactoglucopolysaccharide, a gellan, a glucuronan, a N-acetyl-glucosamine, a N-acet l- heparosan, a hyaluronic acid, a kefiran, a lentinan, a levan, a mauran, a pullulan, a scleroglucan, a schizophyilan, a stewartan, a succinoglycan, a xanthan, a diutan, a welan, a starch, a derivatized starch, a tamarind, a tragacanth, a guar gum, a derivatized guar gum, a gum ghatti, a gum arabic, a locust bean gum, a cellulose, and a derivatized cellulose. 17. The method of any one of claims 1-16, wherein the polysaccharide has a number average molecular weight of about 1,000 MW to about 150,000 MW. 18. The method of any one of claims 1-17, wherein the polysaccharide is dextran. 19. The method of any one of claims 1-18, wherein the dextran has a number average molecular weight of about 1,000 MW to about 150,000 MW. 20. The method of any one of claims 1-19, wherein the cross-linked carbohydrate-based coating is cross-linked. 21. The method of any one of claims 1-20, wherein the cross-linked carbohydrate-based coating is the reaction product of a cross-linking reaction between an epoxide-based compound and a carbohydrate. 22. The method of any one of claims 1-21, wherein the epoxide-based compound is selected from the group consisting of is one or more selected from the group consisting of a polyethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, an epichlorohydrin, a 1 ,4-butanediol diglycidyl ether, an ethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, a 1 ,6-hexanediol diglycidyl ether, a propylene glycol diglycidyl ether, a poly(propylene glycol)diglycidyl ether), a poly(tetramethylene glyeoi)diglyeidyl ether, a neopentyl glycol diglycidyl ether, a polyglycerol polyglycidyl ether, a di glycerol polyglycidyl ether, a glycerol polyglycidyl ether, a trimethylpropane polyglycidyl ether, a l,2-(bis(2,3- epoxypropoxy)ethylene), a pentaerythritol giycidyl ether, a pentaeiythntol polyglycidyl ether, a sorbitol polyglycidyl ether, and mixtures thereof. 23. The method of any one of claims 1-22, wherein the epoxide-based compound is pentaerythritol giycidyl ether. 24. The method of any one of claims 1-23, wherein the cross-linked carbohydrate-based coating is a reaction product of quenching reaction between the cross-linked carbohydrate-based coating and an amine-functionalized compound. 25. The method of any one of claims 1-24, wherein the amine-functionalized compound has the structure: ( R 1 ^ H 2 wherein at each occurrence R 1 at is independently selected from -H, -OH, or a substituted or unsubstituted (Ci-Cio)hyrdocarbyl. 26. The method of any one of claims 1-25, wherein at each occurrence R 1 is independently selected from -H, -OH, or -(Ci-Cio)alkyl-OH. 27. The method of any one of claims 1-26, wherein the amine-functionalized compound is 2- amino-2-hydroxymethyl-propane- 1 ,3 -diol. 28. The method of any one of claims 1-27, wherein the composition further comprises a kosmotropic ion comprising magnesium, lithium, fluoride, sulfate, and mixtures thereof. 29. The method of any one of claims 1-28, further including aggregating and precipitating the coated nanoparticles in the subterranean formation by the addition of a kosmotropic ion. 30. The method of any one of claims 1-29, wherein the method is a method of fluid diversion and further comprises aggregating and precipitating of the coated nanoparticles in the subterranean formation by the addition of a kosmotropic ion. 31. The method of any one of claims 1-30, wherein the method is a method of conformance control and further comprises aggregating and precipitating of the coated nanoparticles in the subterranean formation by the addition of a kosmotropic ion. 32. The method of any one of claims 1-31, wherein the method further comprises aggregating the coated nanoparticles at an oil -water interface. 33. The method of any one of claims 1 -32, wherein the coated nanoparticles are aggregated at one or more oil-water interfaces by the addition of a chaotropic ion. 34. The method of any one of claims 1-33, wherein the coated nanoparticle has a hydrodynamic diameter of 10 nm to 50 nm. 35. The method of any one of claims 1-34, wherein the coated nanoparticle has a hydrodynamic diameter of about 20 nm to about 60 nm. 36. The method of any one of claims 1-35, wherein the coated nanoparticles of the composition have a hydrodynamic diameter of less than about 100 nm after heating at 90 Â°C in seawater for 7 days. 37. The method of any one of claims 1-36, wherein the coated nanoparticles of the composition have a hydrodynamic diameter that is less than the hydrodynamic diameter of similar coated nanoparticles in a similar composition without the ion. 38. The method of any one of claims 1-37, wherein the coated nanoparticles of the composition have a lower critical solution temperature of greater than 90 Â°C. 39. The method of any one of claims 1 -38, wherein the coated nanoparticles of the composition have a higher permeability as compared to similar coated nanoparticles in a similar composition without the ion. 40. The method of any one of claims 1-39, further comprising combining the composition with an aqueous or oil-based fluid comprising a drilling fluid, a stimulation fluid, a fracturing fluid, a spotting fluid, a clean-up fluid, a completion fluid, a remedial treatment fluid, an abandonment fluid, a pill, an acidizing fluid, a cementing fluid, a packer fluid, a logging fluid, or a combination thereof, to form a mixture, wherein the placing the composition in the subterranean formation comprises placing the mixture in the subterranean formation. 41. The method of any one of claims 1-40, wherein at least one of prior to, during, and after the placing of the composition in the subterranean formation, the composition is used in the subterranean formation, at least one of alone and in combination with other materials, as a 

 drilling fluid, a stimulation fluid, a fracturing fluid, a spotting fluid, a clean-up fluid, a completion fluid, a remedial treatment fluid, an abandonment fluid, a pill, an acidizing fluid, a cementing fluid, a packer fluid, a logging fluid, or a combination thereof. 42. The method of any one of claims 1-41, wherein the composition further comprises saline, aqueous base, oil, organic solvent, synthetic fluid oil phase, aqueous solution, alcohol or polyol, cellulose, starch, alkalinity control agent, acidity control agent, density control agent, density modifier, emulsifier, dispersant, polymeric stabilizer, crosslmking agent, polyacrylamide, polymer or combination of polymers, antioxidant, heat stabilizer, foam control agent, diluent, plasticizer, filler or inorganic particle, pigment, dye, precipitating agent, rheology modifier, oil- wetting agent, set retarding additive, surfactant, corrosion inhibitor, gas, weight reducing additive, heavy-weight additive, lost circulation material, filtration control additive, salt, fiber, thixotropic additive, breaker, crossl inker, gas, rheology modifier, curing accelerator, curing retarder, pH modifier, chelating agent, scale inhibitor, enzyme, resin, water control material, polymer, oxidizer, a marker, Portland cement, pozzolana cement, gypsum cement, high alumina content cement, slag cement, silica cement, fly ash, metakaolin, shale, zeolite, a crystalline silica compound, amorphous silica, fibers, a hydratable clay, microspheres, pozzolan lime, or a combination thereof. 43. The method of any one of claims 1 -42, wherein the composition further comprises a proppant, a resin-coated proppant, or a combination thereof. 44. The method of any one of claims 1-43, further comprising processing the composition exiting the annulus with at least one fluid processing unit to generate a cleaned composition and recirculating the cleaned composition through the wellbore. 45. A method of treating a subterranean formation, the method comprising: placing in a subterranean formation a nanoparticle composition comprising: a coated nanoparticle comprising an iron oxide nanoparticle and a cross-linked carbohydrate-based coating comprising dextran, pentaerythritol giycidyl ether, and 2-amino-2- hydroxymethyi-propane- 1 ,3-diol; and 

 an ion comprising Ca " \ wherein the dextran is cross- linked by pentaerythritol glycidyl ether. 46. A system comprising: a nanoparticle composition comprising: a coated nanoparticle comprising a nanoparticle and a cross-lmked carbohydrate- based coating, and an ion selected from the group consisting of Li '1" , Na + , 1" , Rb + ' Cs + , Be 2â , Mg /+ , Ca 2'1" , Sr 2 f , Ba , and mixtures thereof: and a subterranean formation comprising the composition therein. 47. A nanoparticle composition for treatment of a subterranean formation, the nanoparticle composition comprising: a coated nanoparticle comprising a nanoparticle and a cross-linked carbohydrate-based coating, and an ion selected from the group consisting of Li + , Na + , K~, Rb +> Cs + , Be 2+ , Mg 2Ã· , Ca 2+ , Sr + , Ba 2 r , and mixtures thereof. 48. The composition of claim 47, wherein the composition further comprises a downhole fluid. 49. A composition for treatment of a subterranean formation, the composition comprising: a coated nanoparticle comprising an iron oxide nanoparticle and a cross-lmked carbohydrate-based coatmg comprising dextran, pentaerythntol glycidyl ether, and 2-amino-2- hydroxymethyl-propane-l ,3-diol; and an ion comprising Ca 2+ ; wherein the dextran is cross-lmked by pentaerythritol glycidy l ether.
